title: Didymoglossum denticulatum
taxonomy: Didymoglossum denticulatum is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family consists of about 700 species of epiphytic ferns and is commonly known as the 'filmy ferns' family. The Hymenophyllaceae family is part of the larger group of vascular plants called the Pteridophyta. The Pteridophyta is a division of plants that also includes the Ophioglossaceae and Osmundaceae families. intro: Didymoglossum denticulatum, also known as the tooth-leaved moonwort or tooth-leaved polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Didymoglossum denticulatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities.
